name: Computer Networking
goal: Understand how computer networks function from physical to application layer
units: Unit 1: OSI and TCP/IP Models - layers, encapsulation, what each handles
Unit 2: Physical and Data Link - bits, frames, MAC addresses, switches
Unit 3: IP and Routing - IPv4/IPv6, subnets, CIDR, routing tables
Unit 4: TCP and UDP - connection setup, reliability, flow control
Unit 5: DNS - hierarchy, resolution, record types, caching
Unit 6: HTTP and TLS - application layer, HTTPS handshake
Unit 7: NAT and Firewalls - address translation, packet filtering
Unit 8: Diagnostics - ping, traceroute, netstat, Wireshark basics
